en.name: Maintenance drones and robots to enhance renewable energy systems in the Atlantic Area
en.description:
Aerospace and robotic technologies together with composites and additive manufacturing will be interweaved to produce full innovative solutions for a more efficient energy production. The use of support robots on solar panels and windmill engines will boost these clean energies in the area. 
DURABLE will accelerate the deployment of renewable energies in the Atlantic Area through the validation and demonstration of previous developed aerospace technologies applied in robotics for operation and maintenance activities in wind and solar energy systems.
The supporting robots will be adapted to perform tasks of inspection/surveillance and reparation reducing the high costs of these activities favouring regional renewable energy production conditions.
The project will create an innovative institutional and technical cooperation network that will reduce the operation & maintenance costs in the renewable energy production, merging key technological and institutional actors from these critical sectors.
en.expected_results:
The application of robotics and aeronautic disruptive knowledge and technologies to the renewables sector will generate a reduction of O&M costs (automatic inspections repair, safer operations) in a long-lasting way as both sectors are in continuous evolution. 
The co-development of technologies will generate long-lasting relations among sectors so the benefits for renewable energy, but also to robotic and aeronautics, will last during the years allowing the implementation of the innovation of one area into the other. 
The benefits will be demonstrated to end-users at transnational level. Different workshops and working sessions will be held to guarantee the transfer of the exploitation opportunities beyond the lifetime of the project to private sector and local and regional organizations. 
In terms of replicability, EDF Group is owning a large assets fleet in the Atlantic Area and will consider the application of the most promising technologies / solutions across its entire fleet, made out of several hundreds of solar and wind assets in the Atlantic Area.
en.achievements:
“Drones and Maintenance Robots for the Promotion of Renewable Energies” is born as an ambitious project to tackle the needs of the Atlantic Area in terms of competitiveness in renewable technologies and resilience against climate change. Overall, it is an ambitious project whose goal is to reduce the pollution generated by the current methods for producing energy in the wind and solar farms in the Atlantic Area.
Partners in DURABLE are working on the validation and demonstration of aerospace technologies such as aerial robotics or additive manufacturing and their application to organisation and maintenance. With the technologies developed within the project, improvements such as the automation of inspection and repair tasks or the rapid replacement of parts are achieved, reducing costs, and improving the productivity of the new plants. 
Some of the most promising technologies developed by the partners are the following: intelligent navigation technology using drones for high-precision damage assessment and reporting; novel technologies for the inspection of wind turbines with a fleet of drones; a methodology of ultrasonic inspection for wind blades; portable low-power Radio Emission Spectroscopy technology; unmanned Ground Vehicles (land robots equipped with advanced sensing and actuation technologies); and an immersive 4D virtual cockpit for remote operations. The last step of the project will be to implement these technologies into two pilot sites in Portugal and France for their validation. 
"Any technology that will allow industry to remotely check wind and solar farms, and indeed do so on aerial systems that are themselves based on sustainable rechargeable battery technologies, will be a huge bonus in developing Europe’s Green Economy in the Atlantic Area”. Dr. Alessio Di Liberto Electronic Engineer at DCU.
en.expected_outputs:
• Develop a mapping of the needs of solar and wind farms, creating a robotic technology catalogue to detect applications in Operation & Maintenance for renewables. 
• Deploy new applications of UAVs to be tested in several case studies in renewable energies facilities for inspection tasks demonstrating the impact of non-contact monitoring. 
• Deliver of case studies for application of automated composites repair in renewable sector to demonstrate the viability of Additive Manufacturing in maintenance for energy facilities. Identification of key aspects for implementing a demonstration site and analysis of the impact of the use of robotic innovative platforms for Operation & Maintenance operations in solar and wind farms.

pt.name: Drones e robôs de manutenção para a promoção de energias renováveis no Espaço Atlântico
pt.description: DURABLE irá potenciar a utilização das energias renováveis no Espaço Atlântico através da validação e demonstração de tecnologias aeroespaciais aplicadas na área da robótica para suporte e manutenção de sistemas de energia eólica e solar. Os robôs de apoio serão adaptados para executar tarefas de inspeção, monitorização e reparação, reduzindo os elevados custos dessas atividades, favorecendo as condições de produção local de energia renovável

es.name: Drones y robots de mantenimiento para el fomento de las energías renovables en el Espacio Atlántico
es.description: DURABLE va a acelerar el desempeño de las energías renovables en el Espacio Atlántico mediante validación y demostración de tecnologías aeroespaciales previamente desarrolladas, aplicadas en robótica para actividades de operación y mantenimiento en sistemas de energía solar y eólica. Los robots de apoyo se adaptarán para realizar tareas de inspección/vigilancia y reparación reduciendo los altos costes de estas tareas favoreciendo las condiciones regionales de producción de las energías renovables.

fr.name: Drones et robots d'entretien pour la promotion des énergies renouvelables dans la région Atlantique
fr.description: DURABLE accélèrera la performance des énergies renouvelables dans l'Espace Atlantique grâce à la validation et démonstration de technologies aérospatiales déjà développées, appliquées en robotique pour exploitation et maintenance des systèmes d'énergie solaire et éolienne. Les robots de soutien seront adaptés pour effectuer des tâches d'inspection/surveillance et réparation, réduisant les coûts élevés de ces tâches, favorisant les conditions régionales de production d’énergie renouvelable

pt.name: Património cultural imaterial inspirado nas paisagens terrestres e marítimas do Espaço Atlântico
pt.description: AtlanticCultureScapes irá desenvolver experiências comercializáveis baseadas no Património Cultural Imaterial (PCI) para melhorar a vida dos que vivem, trabalham ou visitam o Espaço Atlântico. Uma análise da oferta turística do PCI será realizada para delinear os princípios orientadores para um crescimento económico sustentável. O projeto irá apresentar soluções criativas para problemas comuns e desenvolver mecanismos inovadores para maximizar as oportunidades conjuntas.

es.name: Patrimonio cultural intangible en el entorno terrestre y marino del Espacio Atlántico
es.description: AtlanticCultureScapes desarrollará experiencias susceptibles de ser comercializadas, a partir del concepto de Patrimonio Cultural Intangible (PCI), que mejorarán las vidas de quien vive, trabaja o visita el Espacio Atlántico. Se llevará a cabo un análisis de la oferta turística de PCI con el fin de proporcionar principios rectores para el crecimiento económico sostenible. El proyecto ofrecerá soluciones creativas a problemas comunes y desarrollará mecanismos innovadores para maximizar las oportunidades comunes.

fr.name: Patrimoine culturel immatériel inspiré par les paysages terrestres/maritimes désignés dans l'Espace Atlantique
fr.description: AtlanticCultureScapes développera des expériences vendables ancrées dans le Patrimoine Culturel Immatériel (PCI) pour améliorer la vie de ceux qui vivent, travaillent ou visitent l'Espace Atlantique. Une analyse de l'offre touristique du PCI sera réalisée afin de fournir des principes directeurs pour une croissance économique durable. Le projet fournira des solutions créatives aux problèmes communs et développera des mécanismes innovants pour maximiser les opportunités communes.

en.name: Intangible cultural heritage inspired by designated land and seascapes in the Atlantic Area
en.description:
The Atlantic Area regions have a rich intangible cultural heritage but the ‘intangible’ element of cultural heritage has received relatively little attention in Atlantic Area. 
AtlanticCultureScapes will focus on enhancing the tourism offering through the sustainable integration of Intangible Cultural Heritage (ICH) thereby stimulating increased economic gain from tourism along the Atlantic Area,  which will enhance the lives of those who live, work or visit its regions. 
The project will deliver creative solutions and develop innovative mechanisms to maximise common opportunities, by supporting stakeholders to collaborate in order to offer visitors a truly unique and regional experience reflective of the Atlantic culture, heritage and identity.
The project will generate an inventory of ICH entrepreneurs in partner areas and from this base stimulate proactive collaboration amongst networks of entrepreneurs working in this field and support them in their development. The products will be conglomerated into an Atlantic Area Route, allowing the project to raise the profile of the sector through transnationally organised marketing including publicising the ICH inventory and seeking the involvement of other entrepreneurs across the Atlantic Area.
en.expected_results:
The main cultural results will be:
• Enhanced recognition of ICH as an economic asset worthy of protection by key policymakers at local, national and Atlantic Area levels.
• Enhanced vibrancy in ICH entrepreneurs.
• Greater profile/viability of ICH events and processes across Atlantic Area.
The main community results will be:
• Enhanced valuing by local community of cultural assets.
• Enhanced vibrancy and community cohesion.
The main tourism results will be:
• Enhanced enjoyment of authentic cultural experiences leading to return visits.
• Enhanced integration of ICH sector into the destination and its management.
• Enhanced confidence in ICH entrepreneurs.
en.achievements:
The Atlantic Area regions have a rich intangible cultural heritage. The AtlanticCultureScape project enhances the tourism offering through the sustainable integration of Intangible Cultural Heritage (ICH), thereby stimulating increased economic gain, which improves the lives of those who live, work or visit the Atlantic regions. The aim is to offer visitors a truly unique and regional experience reflective of the Atlantic culture, heritage, and identity. The project delivers creative and innovative solutions to maximise common opportunities, by encouraging networks and entrepreneurs working in this field to collaborate with each other, and assist them in bringing these products to market. Thus, AtlanticCultureScape carried out an inventory of ICH entrepreneurs, which received guidance and integrated the management of destinations and their governance.
A Marketing Plan was also prepared to promote the recognition of ICH as an economic asset worthy of protection by key policymakers at local, national, and Atlantic area levels. The aim is thus to achieve greater value by the local community of cultural assets and enhanced community cohesion. In terms of tourism, it is expected an enhanced enjoyment of authentic cultural experiences leading to return visits and enhanced integration of ICH sector into the destination and its management, resulting as well on enhanced confidence in ICH entrepreneurs. The delivery of an Atlantic Area Route allows thus to raise the profile of the sector through transnationally organised marketing, including publicising the ICH inventory and seeking the involvement of several entrepreneurs across the Atlantic Area.
"Three years ago I had been happily making lace amongst a small group of local Lace-makers and yet I was also acutely aware of the need to do something more and possibly something different to safeguard this beautiful craft from dying out in the locality. The Atlantic Culturescape project couldn’t have been timelier. I knew I was on the right pathway pretty much straight away as the ethos of the programme really spoke to me in terms of the importance of sharing the story of a place through the traditions and the customs of her people” Rosie Finnegan-Bell Lace, Love & Forget-me-nots.
en.expected_outputs:
• Set of Guiding Principles to help establish sustainability within the ICH entrepreneurial sector.
• Inventory of ICH Entrepreneurs within partner/associate partner areas and used for the promotion of ICH and the Route.
• Networks of ICH entrepreneurs signed up to the guidance within each partner area, integrated into destination management and its governance.
• A series of ICH products jointly developed with the networks which pilot the Guiding Principles – learning from pilots to be assessed by Partnership and where appropriate integrated into next version of Principles.
• Jointly produced ICH Marketing Plan to identify key target markets and plan actions.
• Transnational Marketing of ICH Route to identified target markets.

en.name: Water-Energy-Seafood nexus: eco-innovation and circular economy strategues in the Atlantic Area
en.description:
The transition to a circular economy provides opportunities, especially for the food sector. However, in a global food economy, investments and new alliances between regions will be necessary. It is important to balance environmental influences with social and economic gain in policy and decision making for regional and global food flow. 
The NEPTUNUS project aims to promote the sustainable development of the seafood sector in the Atlantic Area by supplying a consistent methodology for products eco-labelling and defining eco-innovation strategies for production and consumption under a circular economy approach. 
Its main objective is to pursue a new transnational clustering concept approach (NEXUS) to review, examine and harness key eco-labelling and key enabling eco-innovations that add-value and cross-cut sea food-water-energy domains in order to address barriers and to strengthen these sectors regionally and across jurisdictions in the Atlantic region. This project will provide key actions for resource efficiency based on life cycle thinking, incorporating producers, policy makers and consumers in the decision making process.
en.expected_results:
The proposal will establish a long-term networking based on two main complementary driving forces: i) institutions with the knowledge and the capacity to define eco-innovation strategies, ii) institutions with the ability to establish contacts with the productive seafood sector for the collection and transfer of data. 
The NEPTUNUS Project will contribute to the territorial balance and economic cohesion of involved regions, since its strategies and the joint development of tools and policies will contribute to balance uneven capacity among peripheral maritime regions and sectors with regard to the issue addressed by the project along its implementation. Thus, is expected that not only the cooperation frame but also the tools developed and validated during the project will continue to be used after its finalisation.
en.achievements:
The transition to a circular economy provides lots of opportunities for the seafood sector. It is important to balance environmental influences with social and economic gain in policy and decision making, to overcome obstacles influencing producers and consumers behaviour in order to consider the importance of natural resources.
The NEPTUNUS project aims to promote the sustainable development of the seafood sector in the Atlantic Area by supplying a consistent methodology for products eco-labelling and defining eco-innovation strategies for production and consumption under a circular economy approach. The consortium thus worked to deliver green economy strategies to the seafood sector to minimise environmental impacts while incorporating competitive products into green markets.
The approach also involved developing strategies and policies based on circular economy and cradle-to-cradle principles that deliver new products and working systems to the seafood sector, to reduce the resources used in processes by recycling and valorising the waste outputs into production and consumption systems.
As main results, NEPTUNUS published 15 scientific papers and has built a robust database for seafood supply chains, introduced technical, environmental and nutritional criteria under the life cycle inventories, promoting a continuous improvement of the environmental performance of seafood throughout their life cycle. Besides, an analytic tool is being developed to support stakeholders to estimate the impact of their activity (fishing, aquaculture, or processing).
All these results will benefit industries by means of a new green eco-labelling, public authorities by providing pilot studies to contribute to the economic cohesion of involved regions, research organisations by participating in an ambitious strategy and consumers by informing them about the environmental benefits of sustainable products. 
"The perpetuation of fishing practices from an environmentally, socially, and economically sustainable approach is essential to guarantee not only the future of coastal populations, but also the supply of seafood to society and the safeguarding of cultural heritage. Reconciling all of this with the protection of marine biodiversity, which is an unavoidable duty in any society, as set out in the United Nations 2030 Agenda for Sustainable Development.” Sandra Ceballos Researcher.
en.expected_outputs:
The main outputs will be those strategies inform and underpin production and consumption of seafood where activities will be pursued, managed and communicated under a Nexus clustering concept approach to promote and maximise regional and cross-jurisdictional economic growth in the Atlantic area.
• Strategies related to Water-Energy-Seafood Nexus footprint.
• Strategies related to Circular Economy in the Seafood sector.
• Strategies to address the main challenges of the fisheries in the Atlantic area, including the adaptation to the climate change and the growing threats of the marine debris.

pt.name: Nexo Água-Energia-Pescado:  estratégias de eco-inovação e economia circular no Espaço Atlântico
pt.description: O projeto NEPTUNUS visa promover o desenvolvimento sustentável do setor do pescado no Espaço Atlântico através da disponibilização de uma metodologia para a rotulagem ecológica de produtos e da definição de estratégias de eco-inovação para a produção e o consumo, no contexto da economia circular. Deste projeto resultarão ações-chave para o uso eficiente de recursos baseadas no pensamento de ciclo de vida e integrando produtores, decisores políticos e consumidores no processo de tomada de decisão.

fr.name: Nexus Eau-Énergie-Pêche: stratégies d'eco-innovation et économie circulaire dans l'Espace Atlantique
fr.description: NEPTUNUS vise à promouvoir le développement durable du secteur de la pêche et des fruits de mer dans l’ Atlantique en fournissant une méthodologie pour l'affichage environnemental des produits et en fixant des stratégies d'éco-innovation pour la production et la consommation, fondées sur l'économie circulaire. Ce projet permettra l'utilisation efficace des ressources, basées sur la pensée du cycle de vie et intégrer les producteurs, les consommateurs et les décideurs dans la prise de décision.

es.name: Nexus-Agua-Energía, Pescado: estratégias de inovación y economía circular en el Espacio Atlántico
es.description: El proyecto NEPTUNUS tiene como objetivo promover el desarrollo sostenible del sector pesquero en el Espacio Atlántico por medio del desarrollo de una metodología para el eco-etiquetado de productos y la definición de estrategias de eco-innovación, bajo un enfoque de economía circular. El proyecto facilitará herramientas para la eficiencia de los recursos basadas en el pensamiento de ciclo de vida, incorporando a productores, consumidores y gestores públicos en el proceso de toma de decisiones.

en.name: Prevention / mitigation of mycotoxin contamination of food / feed by climate change
en.description:
Food and feed industries have a need for innovative analytic toxicity prevention strategies (e.g. toxicity alert systems and risk management plans) to produce safer food products for humans and animals.
AGRITOX aims to provide the Atlantic Area food and feed industries with information and technical solutions to avoid the contamination by mycotoxins, which is a growing challenge due to the climate change and represent a major concern for human health. Thus, AGRITOX project will develop toxicity alert systems and risk management plans at disposal of the industry. These will facilitate the adoption of safer uses by the companies, namely SMEs, not only for a better and safer cultivation, harvest and storage of human food and animal feed, but also for achieving safer food products for the final consumer, thus covering the whole value chain.
en.expected_results: Improving the competitiveness of SMEs in the agri-food and livestock sectors in the Atlantic Area, and identifying the safest and most innovative products along the value chain through the transfer of emerging technologies in early detection of mycotoxin contamination and its eventual mitigation.
en.achievements:
Mycotoxins are compounds produced by filamentous fungi, some of which are known to be toxic to humans and animals. Mycotoxin contamination impacts, not only  human health, but also farm businesses, and this constitutes a growing challenge due to climate change and the increase of temperatures it brings with it. Food and feed industries need innovative analytic toxicity prevention strategies to produce safer food products for humans and animals. The AGRITOX project aims to provide these Atlantic Area industries with information and technical solutions to avoid the contamination by mycotoxins. The project is focused on the entire value chain considering the presence of regulated, emerging and modified mycotoxins in food and feed. Partners have been working on developing toxicity alert systems and risk management plans that are at the industry’s disposal. They also developed studies on the different strategies for detoxification, such as the efficacy of binders, technological treatments, and nanotechnology. In this way, specific mitigation measures will be adopted. Different partners have developed new methods for the detection of mycotoxins, while others have advanced by performing a literature review of existing analytical techniques. The project created a database with different detection methods that can be used by the industry, according to their needs. Current detection and detoxification methods are being improved in order to guarantee the highest level of food safety.
AGRITOX will result in alert methods and risk management plans that will be available to the industry, effective and feasible. It will facilitate the adoption of safer uses by the companies, not only for better and safer cultivation, harvest and storage of human food and animal feed, but also for achieving safer food products for the final consumer, thus covering the whole value chain.
"The Agritox project is conveying to all the agents involved in the food chain the need to establish mycotoxin analysis protocols and, at the same time, a mycotoxin alert network is being built. In this way, the quality standards of food produced in the Atlantic Area will be highly increased avoiding health risks to consumers.” Luis Botana Universidad de Santiago de Compostela.
en.expected_outputs:
- A joint AGRITOX Toxicity Alert System for the prevention and mitigation of mycotoxins contamination to be used by the food and feed industry, ensuring the involvement of all stakeholders along the value chain within the AA and beyond.
- Piloting the AGRITOX Tocity Alert System in at least 5 industries located in 5 different regions in Atlantic Area to validate the usefulness and feasibility of the system. The project will start with industries from food and feed sectors, depending on Regional agreements of partners with specific involved companies.
- Evaluated risks of mycotoxin contamination in the Atlantic Area for different types of food and feed and developed new risk management plans for each industry involved.
- Emerging and masked mycotoxin database in high-risk products, to facilitate easier identification of toxicity by regulatory authorities as well as further elaboration of public toxicity risk maps.
- Publication of the document: “Prevention toxicity Alert System for food and feed industries”, as an exhaustive guidelines for detecting mycotoxins.
- Recommendation of a regulatory framework.

fr.name: Prévention / atténuation de contamination des aliments par mycotoxines causée par le changement climat
fr.description: AGRITOX vise à fournir aux industries de l'alimentation humaine et animale dans l'Espace Atlantique des informations et solutions techniques pour éviter la contamination par mycotoxines, un défi croissant en raison du changement climatique et une préoccupation pour la santé humaine. Le projet AGRITOX développera des systèmes d'alerte de toxicité et des plans de gestion des risques à la disposition de l'industrie, pour assurer une livraison plus sûre des produits aux consommateurs, avec l'adoption de nouvelles méthodes de culture, récolte et stockage plus sûrs.

es.name: Prevención / mitigación de contaminación por micotoxinas de alimentos / piensos por el cambio climático
es.description: El objetivo de AGRITOX es proporcionar a las industrias de alimentos y piensos del Espacio Atlántico información y soluciones para evitar la contaminación por microtoxinas, reto creciente debido al cambio climático y que representa un problema para la salud. El proyecto AGRITOX desarrollará sistemas de alerta de toxicidad y planes de gestión de riesgos para la industria para asegurar la distribución de productos seguros, con nuevos métodos para el cultivo, cosecha y almacenamiento más seguros.

pt.name: Prevenção/mitigação da contaminação por micotoxinas de alimentos causada pelas alterações climáticas
pt.description: AGRITOX visa fornecer informação e soluções técnicas à indústria alimentar e de rações do Espaço Atlântico para evitar a contaminação por microtoxinas, um desafio crescente devido às alterações climáticas e uma preocupação para a saúde humana. AGRITOX desenvolverá sistemas de alerta de toxicidade e planos de gestão do risco que disponibilizará à indústria para garantir produtos mais seguros para os consumidores, facilitando a adoção de métodos mais seguros de cultivo, colheita e armazenamento.

themes.title: Managing natural and man-made threats, risk management

en.name: Risk assessment and mitigation of the presence of Nanomateirales in Atlantic aquaculture
en.description:
As metallic NPs present important improvements in diverse industrial applications, the frequency of their application is growing exponentially. However, the studies of risks and mitigation of the presence of NPs in the environment are lagging far behind the rate of utilisation, which represents a critical environmental and safety challenge in the Atlantic Area. 
The objective of NANOCULTURE is to advance in knowledge, risk assessment and mitigation of environmental presence of the most-used engineered nanoparticles (ENPs): titanium dioxide (TiO2) and silver (Ag) in market products. 
The focus of the project are the aquatic ecosystems related to aquaculture, specifically, the organisms for human consumption (cultured fish, molluscs, seaweed, sea urchins, etc) a sector of high economic relevance in the Atlantic Area. NANOCULTURE will investigate the effects of ENPs on aquaculture products, their bioaccumulation and assess its impact in human intake.
In order to carry out this project, the collaboration of all the participating centres is essential to ensure a wide range of industrial (aquaculture professionals) and scientific profiles (analytical chemists, physical chemists, molecular biologists,) as well as providing infrastructure to run the analysis and deliver real samples from aquaculture plants.
en.expected_results:
Expected results:
1. Development of standard nanometric procedures. 
2. Information on toxicity of the NPs and protocols of action. 
3. Information on food safety of aquaculture products. 
4. Sensors for the rapid detection of metallic NPs in the waters of aquaculture facilities. 
Products 1, 2 and 3 related to the development of new analytical methods will be used first by the entities participating in the project, but will then be published so that they can be used by other researchers or companies as they are considered as advances in the knowledge that should be of common interest. However, the products included in section 4 related to the specific development of sensors for the rapid detection of metallic NPs in the aquaculture facilities themselves will be the subject of a patent.
en.achievements:
Given the importance of the aquaculture sector for the Atlantic Area, possible adverse effects of the use of engineered nanoparticles in aquaculture should be well understood in order to ensure maximum safety of food products and the environmental-related impacts of the activity. Therefore, the NANOCULTURE project aims to advance in knowledge, risk assessment, and mitigation of the environmental presence of two of the most-used engineered nanoparticles in market products: titanium dioxide and silver.
NANOCULTURE is being carried out primarily with real samples destined for human consumption. The selected species, which are common in the Atlantic Area aquaculture industry, are turbot, mussels, and seaweed. Since these organisms are quite distinct, responses to nanoparticle exposure (water filtration capacities, physiological effects, uptake, and elimination profiles) will also be different.
The project has studied the current situation on the presence, transformation, bioaccumulation, and effects of titanium dioxide and silver in aquaculture products. It has also evaluated the human risk of exposure by oral intake of aquaculture products containing these identified nanoparticles, taking into account the exposure route and the transformations derived from food preparation processes (cooking).
Moreover, it has developed sensors for the rapid identification and quantification of titanium dioxide and silver in aquaculture facilities, allowing decentralised monitoring and quick implementation of measures for risk mitigation. These nanoparticle sensors will enable Atlantic aquaculture to make use of the digital revolution and provide real-time on-site monitoring.
The results achieved by NANOCULTURE can be grouped into three categories: i) advancement in knowledge related to the toxicity of engineered nanoparticles to aquaculture organisms, ii) presentation of recommendations and standard operating procedures for mitigating the risk of nanoparticles in aquaculture products, and iii) the development of new analytical methods for detection, characterisation, and quantification of metallic nanoparticles in different media.
en.expected_outputs:
- 	Standard Operation Procedures on the extraction and analysis of NPs from aquaculture products.
- 	Report on the concentration and size ranges of NPs present in the aquaculture species, as well as on their localization in the organs/tissues.
- 	New proteomic data with biochemical and metabolic information about the effects of NPs on aquaculture species.
- 	Recommendation on the regulation of the presence of NPs in aquaculture.
- 	Report on the determination and characterization of NPs in the bio-accessible and bioavailable fraction in aquaculture products.
- 	Report on the effect of cooking food on the bioavailability of NPs.
- 	Recommendation on the safety limits of NPs in products from aquaculture for human consumption.
- 	Report on the transformations of NPs on water used in aquaculture.
- 	First generation sensors for in situ determination of NPs in water for aquaculture.
- 	Report on the validation of the sensors for in situ determination of NPs in water for aquaculture.
- 	Standard Operation Procedures for mitigation of the risks associated to the presence of NPs in aquaculture products.

es.name: Evaluación y mitigación de riesgos de la presencia de Nanomateirales en aquacultura atlántica
es.description: El objetivo de NANOCULTURE es profundizar en el conocimiento, evaluación y mitigación de riesgos ambientales de las nanopartículas de ingeniería más utilizadas (ANP): dióxido de titanio (TiO2) y plata (Ag) en productos de mercado. El foco del proyecto están los ecosistemas relacionados con la acuicultura, un sector de alta relevancia económica en el Espacio Atlántico. NANOCULTURE investigará los efectos de las ANP en productos acuícolas, su bioacumulación y evaluará su impacto en la ingesta humana.

pt.name: Avaliação  e mitigação de riscos da presença de Nanomateriais na área da aquicultura Atlântica
pt.description: O objetivo da NANOCULTURE é aprofundar o conhecimento, avaliação e mitigação dos riscos ambientais das nanopartículas de engenharia mais utilizadas (ANP): dióxido de titânio (TiO2) e prata (Ag) em produtos de mercado. O foco do projeto está nos ecossistemas aquáticos relacionados com a aquicultura, um setor de grande importância económica na área do Atlântico. O NANOCULTURE investigará os efeitos das ANP em produtos de aquacultura e a sua bioacumulação e avaliará o seu impacto na ingestão humana.

fr.name: Évaluation des risques de la présence des Nanomatériaux dans les aquacultures atlantiques
fr.description: L'objectif de NANOCULTURE est l'évaluation des risques et l'atténuation de la présence environnementale des nanoparticules d'ingénierie plus utilisées (ENP): dioxyde de titane (TiO2) et argent (Ag) dans produits commerciaux. Ce projet focalise ses activités principales sur les aquacultures, un secteur d'une grande puissance économique dans l'Espace Atlantiq. NANOCULTURE étudiera les effets des ENP sur des produits de l'aquaculture et leur bioaccumulation et évaluera leur impact sur l'ingestion humaine.

themes.title: Managing natural and man-made threats, risk management

en.name: Smart and eco-innovative seafood processes and products for healthy ageing
en.description:
SEAFOOD-AGE tackles a common challenge in the Atlantic Area: an ageing population. Older adults often suffer from illness or disability with the subsequent socioeconomic implications. Healthy ageing requires a healthy diet, and seafood products provide essential nutrients not always accessible to older adults.
SEAFOOD-AGE will exploit the maritime dimension of the Atlantic Area regions and will adopt circular economy concepts to generate ready-to-eat seafood for healthy ageing, produce novel eco-packaging and develop a smart label for better quality, safety and minimum food waste.
With focus on the 65+adults' needs, the project will:
 - design sustainable, healthier, safer, high-quality ready-to-eat seafood products,
 - produce eco-innovative packages exploiting natural resources and by-products,
 - design smart predictive labels to aid retailers to reduce food waste , and to support ageing consumers to make better-informed ready-to-eat seafood choices, and
 - develop competences for innovation and accelerate the pace of embedding the circular economy principles into the seafood sector.
en.expected_results:
Main results include:
• Healthy ageing in the Atlantic Area and beyond, providing older adults access to essential nutrients, thus helping to reduce malnutrition and the associated health burdens.
• Efficient and sustainable use of resources, embedding the circular economy principles in the seafood and packaging sectors.
• Higher quality, safer supply chain and reduced food waste, adopting smart predictive labels will facilitate higher quality, safer cold chain. 
• Better innovation profile and competitiveness in the AA. Improved cooperation will enhance the uptake of project innovations and will result in high added-value and more competitive seafood sector.
en.achievements:
The ageing of population is a common challenge in the Atlantic Area. Healthy ageing requires a healthy diet and seafood products provide valuable nutritional properties, not always accessible to older adults. However, the efficient and sustainable use of marine resources poses further challenges. SEAFOOD-AGE project has exploited the maritime dimension of the Atlantic regions and has adopted circular economy concepts to design sustainable, healthier, safer and high quality ready-to-eat marine products, with a special focus on meeting the needs of adults over 65 years of age and reduce food loss. Strategies have been designed to optimise the production of functional ingredients with high nutritional and healthy properties from underexploited biomass, to be incorporated into ready-to-eat seafood-based products in
the form of fish fillets, fishballs or any other shape. Eco-innovative packaging solutions from marine resources or by-products of the food industry were also developed, as edible and biodegradable sachets for sauces or coatings that prevent decomposition of the product by microbial growth.
Another action of SEAFOOD-AGE was the development of a smart predictive label (based on tracking the temperature through the value chain and working with algorithms on the cloud that can predict the actual shelflife of the product) to help retailers reduce food waste while optimizing product quality and safety and support consumers to make betterinformed seafood choices. SEAFOOD-AGE promoted the collaboration among research centres and industrial partners across the Atlantic regions and also promoted competencies for innovation to increase SMEs competitiveness and accelerate the pace of embedding the circular economy principles into the seafood sector.
"The 30 volunteers over 65 years of age who participated in the tasting held in Ourense, were really excited to be part of an innovative experience and to taste this food for the first time. Their satisfaction at participating in a tasting-test as potential consumers were unanimous, most of them asking when the product would be on sale to the public so that they could consume it”. Juan Ramón Álvarez Blanco President of the Fundación Dorzán.
en.expected_outputs: Outputs have been planned to ensure consistency with project aims and will contribute to the programme’s output indicators. Main outputs in WP1-WP3 include the partnership agreement; communication and capitalisation means and plans; pilot actions and final conference. Each thematic WP foresees several outputs including functional ingredients, RTE seafood for adults 65+, eco-packages and the smart predictive label. Besides joint technical and scientific publications will be produced.

pt.name: Processos e produtos da pesca inteligentes, ecológicos e inovadores para o envelhecimento saudável
pt.description:
SEAFOOD-AGE aborda um desafio socio-económico comum no Espaço Atlântico: o envelhecimento da população. O envelhecimento saudável requer uma dieta benéfica e os produtos do mar fornecem nutrientes essenciais que nem sempre estão acessíveis às pessoas mais velhas.
O projecto visa explorar a dimensão marítima das regiões do Espaço Atlântico e adoptar conceitos de economia circular para gerar marisco pronto a comer para um envelhecimento saudável, produzir novas embalagens ecológicas e desenvolver um rótulo inteligente para melhor qualidade, segurança e mínimo desperdício de alimentos.

es.name: Procesos y productos de la pesca inteligentes y eco-innovadores para un envejecimiento saludable
es.description:
SEAFOOD-AGE aborda el reto socio-económico del envejecimiento poblacional en el Espacio Atlántico. El envejecimiento saludable requiere una dieta saludable y los alimentos marinos ofrecen nutrientes esenciales no siempre accesibles a las personas mayores.
El proyecto explotará la dimensión marítima de las regiones del Espacio Atlántico y adoptará conceptos de economía circular para generar productos del mar listos para el consumo para un envejecimiento saludable, producir nuevos envases ecológicos y desarrollar una etiqueta inteligente para una mejor calidad, seguridad y un mínimo desperdicio de alimentos.

fr.name: Procédés et produits marins intelligents et éco-innovants pour un vieillissement en santé
fr.description:
SEAFOOD-AGE aborde le défi socio-économique du vieillissement de la population dans l’Espace Atlantique. Le vieillissement en santé exige une alimentation saine et les produits marins fournissent nutriments essentiels auxquels les personnes âgées ne sont pas toujours accessibles.
Le projet exploitera la dimension maritime des régions de l’Espace Alantique et adoptera des concepts d’économie circulaire pour générer des produits de la mer prêts à consommer pour un vieillissement en bonne santé, produire de nouveaux emballages écologiques et développer une étiquette intelligente pour une meilleure qualité, sécurité et un minimum de gaspillage alimentaire.
